best_round.title: An Adequate Substitute for Sex Ed

voters.name: Nate
voters.comment: On tessellate I think I commented that it was not in my top 5 alt-j songs. This is #2

voters.name: Saa
voters.comment: Not sure if you selected this because of "Get Better," but that song definitely deserved more votes. :( It still makes me cry.Breezeblocks on the other hand? Yeah, still a banger!! But I also think An Awesome Wave is an album with zero misses. I've always loved the reference to Where the Wild Things Are, too.

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: I haven't listened to this one in a while. I blame that on you being such an Alt-J fan that you prefer the deeper cuts.

voters.name: Lia
voters.comment: I really liked the vibes of this! The da da da da part 👌

voters.name: Mike
voters.comment: I didn't think alt-j ever really did poorly but asking Eric I remember Get Better now. That was a powerful song, that just wasn't a necessarily enjoyable song to listen to. In the right round it would have probably done a lot better, for the general 2020s round though I think that's why it struggled. Anyway I do enjoy Breezeblocks but am low on points and this is the 5th alt-j.

voters.name: Steve
voters.comment: I was so excited to see some alt-J in the playlist! Their song "Something Good" plays in Life is Strange and I randomly sing it all the time. I do agree with the first comment, it's amazing how dramatic they make such a silly topic sound.

voters.name: Saa
voters.comment: Love alt-j but that info sold me

voters.name: Mike
voters.comment: Oh ∆, you weird and beautiful people.

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: There! I listened to the ending! Happy?!

voters.name: Sarah
voters.comment: No but it literally sounds like background music to some pagan ritual at the beginning

voters.name: [Left the League]
voters.comment: Ooooh, I really dislike the sound of people eating and drinking, especially that close to a mic. I am so sorry. :(

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: Didn't expect alt-J to come in with the soulful guitar ballad.

voters.name: Saa
voters.comment: Hi Amanda, I loved this. It broke my heart.

voters.name: Lia
voters.comment: Well damn that's sad but beautiful

voters.name: Saa
voters.comment: LOVED this vibe. I listen to a lot of instrumental stuff and this hit just right. I've already started checking out their other stuff.

voters.name: Steve
voters.comment: I definitely feel what you mean about the calming ocean sounds. This is a nice one.

voters.name: Mike
voters.comment: Opal is such an interesting choice for color because it's not just a single color, but is definitely being used as a color descriptor here.

voters.name: Sarah
voters.comment: This is super relaxing to listen to, especially when your dog isn't barking continually in the background

voters.name: Saa
voters.comment: Still really enjoying slenderbodies whenever I come across them.

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: I liked opal ocean a lot but this one is aight.

voters.name: Ola
voters.comment: this one FEELS the dampest
